221	% and the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance, in
222	% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (L48, L57)
223	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean).
224	% SOURCE QUOTE:
225	% "lemma ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W
226	%     [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
227	%     (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
228	%   .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
229	%     simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
230	%     intro Φ
231	%     rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
232	%     infer_instance)"
233	\textit{Source: [Mathlib],
234	\texttt{ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal\_W}, in
235	\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean}, L48--L57.}
236	This is the \emph{template} for the proof technique used in route~(e), but it does
237	\emph{not} apply directly to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\): modules over a
238	\emph{varying} ring are not of the form \(C^{op} \to A\) for a fixed monoidal
239	\(A\).
240	
241	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e), superseded.} The substrate
242	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
243	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
244	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
245	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Route~(e)
246	proposed to obtain a \emph{full} monoidal structure on
247	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
248	by applying \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i),
249	which would require the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (its two
250	whiskering-stability fields \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, with load-bearing
251	local-injectivity half \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}). This is
252	\emph{not} how the group law is realized. The group law consumes only the
253	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms, and the associator is built
254	directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through
255	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}); it never invokes
256	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), a coherent monoidal category, or the stalkwise
257	whiskering apparatus. The route~(e) instance is therefore vestigial and off the
258	critical path.
259	
260	The stalk-infrastructure analysis that route~(e) turned on is recorded here for
261	reference only, since that route is superseded. The \emph{stalk module structure}
262	is already present in Mathlib: the file
263	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk} supplies, for a topological
264	space \(X\), a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on \(X\) and a point
265	\(x \in X\), the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure on the stalk
266	\(\mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) of a presheaf of modules \(M\),
267	together with the germ--scalar compatibility \(\mathtt{germ\_smul}\); a linearity
268	packaging of the induced stalk map of a morphism was built project-side
269	(\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}, the \emph{linearity half} of ingredient (d.1)).
270	Route~(e) additionally required a (d.1)-\emph{bridge} between the site-level
271	\(J.W\) and the topological stalkwise-isomorphism criterion on
272	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
273	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor; there is
274	moreover no monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) in Mathlib. Because the group law
275	is realized through the direct-gluing associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
276	via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) and never through
277	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), none of this stalk apparatus is on the critical path;
278	it is retained only to document the superseded route.
279	
280	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
281	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
282	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
283	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
284	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
285	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
286	independently of any upstream timing.
287	
288	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
289	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
290	
291	\begin{definition}
292	
293	\leanok
294	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
295	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
296	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
297	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
298	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
299	  \[
300	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
301	  \]
302	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
303	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
304	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
305	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
306	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
307	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
308	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
309	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
310	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
311	  canonical compatibility
312	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
313	     \;\cong\;
314	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
315	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
316	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
317	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
318	
319	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
320	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
321	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
322	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
323	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
324	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
325	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
326	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
327	\end{definition}
328	
329	\begin{lemma}
330	
331	\leanok
332	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
333	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
334	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
335	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
336	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
337	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
338	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
339	  \[
340	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
341	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
342	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
343	  \]
344	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
345	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
346	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
347	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
348	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
349	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
350	  \[
351	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
352	    \qquad
353	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
354	  \]
355	  the associator
356	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
357	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
358	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
359	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
360	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
361	\end{lemma}
362	
363	\begin{proof}
364	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
365	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
366	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
367	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
368	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
369	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
370	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
371	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
372	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
373	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
374	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
375	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
376	\end{proof}
377	
378	\begin{remark}
379	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
380	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
381	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
382	        lem:whisker_of_W}
383	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
384	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
385	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
386	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
387	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
388	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
389	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
390	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
391	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
392	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
393	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
394	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
395	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
396	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
397	
398	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
399	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
400	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
401	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
402	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
403	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
404	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
405	  fixed-base presheaves in
406	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
407	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
408	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
409	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). But this
410	  apparatus is not on the critical path at all: the group law is realized without
411	  it.
412	
413	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
414	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
415	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
416	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
417	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
418	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
419	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
420	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
421	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
422	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
423	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
424	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
425	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
426	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
427	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
428	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
429	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
430	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
431	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
432	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
433	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
434	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
435	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
436	\end{remark}
437	
438	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
439	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
440	
441	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
442	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
443	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
444	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
445	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
446	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
447	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
448	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
449	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
450	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
451	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
452	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
453	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
454	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
455	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
456	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
457	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
458	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
459	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
460	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
461	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
462	to the off-path route~(e).
463	
464	\begin{lemma}
465	
466	\leanok
467	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
468	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
469	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
470	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
471	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
472	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
473	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
474	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
475	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
476	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
477	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
478	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
479	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
480	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
481	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
482	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
483	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
484	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
485	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
486	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
487	  \[
488	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
489	      \;\;:=\;\;
490	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
491	  \]
492	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
493	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
494	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
495	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
496	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
497	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
498	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
499	\end{lemma}
500	
501	\begin{proof}
502	
503	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
504	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
505	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
506	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
507	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
508	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
509	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
510	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
511	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
512	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
513	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
514	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
515	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
516	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
517	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
518	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
519	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
520	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
521	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
522	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
523	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
524	
525	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
526	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
527	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
528	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
529	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
530	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
531	\end{proof}
532	
533	\begin{lemma}
534	
535	\leanok
536	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
537	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
538	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
539	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
540	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
541	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
542	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
543	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
544	  morphism
545	  \[
546	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
547	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
548	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
549	  \]
550	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
551	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
552	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
553	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
554	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
555	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
556	\end{lemma}
557	
558	\begin{proof}
559	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
560	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
561	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
562	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
563	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
564	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
565	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
566	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
567	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
568	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
569	  isomorphism}
570	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
571	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
572	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
573	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
574	  therefore extension of scalars along
575	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
576	
577	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
578	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
579	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
580	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
581	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
582	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
583	  the abstract pullback functor.
584	
585	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
586	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
587	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
588	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
589	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
590	   \;\cong\;
591	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
592	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
593	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
594	  comparison
595	  \[
596	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
597	      \;\longrightarrow\;
598	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
599	      \;\otimes\;
600	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
601	  \]
602	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
603	
604	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
605	  % \ref{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback} (the pair $(f^*, f_*)$ of
606	  % pullback/pushforward of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism of ringed
607	  % spaces is an adjoint pair)
608	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L252-273).
609	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
610	  % "\begin{lemma}
611	  % \label{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}
612	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$
613	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces.
614	  % \begin{enumerate}
615	  % \item The functor
616	  % $f_* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y)$
617	  % is left exact. In fact it commutes with all limits.
618	  % \item The functor
619	  % $f^* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$
620	  % is right exact. In fact it commutes with all colimits.
621	  %  [\dots]
622	  % \end{enumerate}
623	  % \end{lemma}
624	  %  [\dots]
625	  % Parts (1) and (2) hold because $(f^*, f_*)$ is an adjoint pair
626	  % of functors, see
627	  % Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules}"
628	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
629	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
630	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the
631	  abstract universal property on which the comparison below rests.}
632	
633	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual: H1 alone).} Completing this comparison at the
634	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
635	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
636	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
637	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
638	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
639	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
640	
641	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
642	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
643	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
644	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
645	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
646	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
647	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
648	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
649	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
650	  \otimes_S N)\).
651	
652	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
653	  \[
654	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
655	  \]
656	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
657	  presheaf-level adjunction
658	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
659	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
660	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
661	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
662	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
663	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
664	  composing the resulting comparison with
665	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.
666	
667	  This presheaf-level adjunction is the presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
668	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
669	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
670	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\). These two building blocks are \emph{Mathlib-absent}
671	  at the pinned commit: only the \emph{sheaf}-level versions exist
672	  (\texttt{Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}), whereas
673	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardId}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) do already exist at the
674	  presheaf level. This residual is being constructed \emph{directly}, mirroring the
675	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
676	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).
677	
678	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
679	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
680	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
681	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
682	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
683	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
684	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
685	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
686	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
687	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is
688	  the \emph{single open substrate obligation} of this chapter. The superseded
689	  route~(e) \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory}\)
690	  (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}) remains named only as a last-resort fallback should the
691	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.
692	\end{proof}
693	
694	\begin{lemma}
695	[Presheaf-level pushforward adjunction and strong-monoidal restriction (H1/H2 substrate)]
696	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}
697	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans,
698	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr,
699	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj,
700	        PresheafOfModules.isIso_of_isIso_app,
701	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}
702	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
703	  These five presheaf-level declarations are the two ingredients on which the
704	  linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} rests; both are absent from Mathlib at
705	  the pinned commit and are supplied project-side.
706	
707	  \emph{H1 (the pushforward adjunction).} For a morphism \(\beta\) of presheaves of
708	  rings, \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\) are the
709	  presheaf-level de-sheafifications of Mathlib's sheaf-level
710	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
711	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr}\)
712	  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}),
713	  reproduced app-for-app one level down at the presheaf of modules. From them
714	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) assembles the presheaf-level adjunction
715	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), the
716	  presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
717	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\). Pairing this against the
718	  existing \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\) through
719	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) yields the canonical isomorphism
720	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) of two left
721	  adjoints of the same pushforward --- exactly the residual identified in Step~3 of
722	  the proof of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
723	
724	  \emph{H2 (strong-monoidal restriction).} \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_app}\) is the
725	  sectionwise isomorphism criterion: a morphism of presheaves of modules is an
726	  isomorphism once each of its section maps is. Built on it,
727	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\) equips the presheaf-level
728	  restriction-of-scalars functor
729	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) with a \emph{strong}-monoidal
730	  structure whenever the base-change comparison \(\alpha\) is \emph{sectionwise
731	  bijective} --- the presheaf-level lift of the ModuleCat-level H2 core
732	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}, assembled section-by-section via
733	  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal}\). It is to be distinguished from the
734	  module-level, ring-equivalence form
735	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv}\) already pinned in
736	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}: the present declaration is the
737	  \emph{presheaf}-level, \emph{bijective}-comparison form, the one the substrate lift
738	  actually consumes (where the comparison map
739	  \((\alpha.\mathtt{app}\,X).\mathtt{hom}\) is a bijective \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)
740	  morphism but is not syntactically a \((\,\cdot\,)\!.\mathtt{toRingHom}\)).
741	
742	  Together H1 and H2 are the two Mathlib-absent ingredients the linchpin needed; they
743	  are self-contained presheaf-level results and are natural upstream-PR candidates.
744	\end{lemma}
745	
746	\begin{lemma}
747	
748	\leanok
749	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
750	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
751	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
752	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
753	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
754	  product: the canonical map
755	  \[
756	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
757	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
758	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
759	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
760	  \]
761	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
762	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
763	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
764	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
765	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
766	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
767	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
768	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
769	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
770	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.
771	\end{lemma}
772

1	# Analogy: SheafOfModules morphism descent — does it exist in Mathlib, and does the descent re-route of `exists_tensorObj_inverse` / `tensorObj_assoc_iso` avoid the abandoned "d.2" stalk-⊗ gap?
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ts226descent
8	
9	## Iteration
10	226
11	
12	## Question
13	For the by-hand `Pic X` group over the varying `𝒪_X`: (A) does Mathlib give morphism
14	gluing/descent for `SheafOfModules` (compatible local homs ⇒ unique global hom)? (B) a
15	"locally-iso ⇒ iso" criterion for sheaf-of-module morphisms? (C) dual/internal-hom commuting
16	with open-immersion restriction? (D) does building `exists_tensorObj_inverse` and
17	`tensorObj_assoc_iso` via A+B+C genuinely AVOID the abandoned stalk-⊗ commutation ("d.2"), or
18	does it secretly re-require a stalkwise/filtered-colimit-⊗ statement?
19	
20	## Project artifact(s)
21	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:1822` — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (CLOSED, d.2-free).
22	- `:1715` — `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (built through the OPEN `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`, route (d)).
23	- `:1955` — `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (sorry; `Linv := dual L` now nameable since the dual landed iter-225).
24	- `:1912` — `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (CLOSED — the template the descent route mirrors).
25	
26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision A: morphism gluing/descent for `SheafOfModules`
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: morphism gluing for `Sheaf J A` is FULLY present in
31	  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`:
32	  - `CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type` (SheafHom.lean:46) — the hom presheaf
33	    `U ↦ (F|_{Over U} ⟶ G|_{Over U})`.
34	  - `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom : IsSheaf J G → IsSheaf J (presheafHom F G)`
35	    (SheafHom.lean:207) — **the hom presheaf into a sheaf is itself a sheaf** (this is the
36	    descent statement: morphisms are determined locally AND a compatible family glues).
37	  - `CategoryTheory.sheafHom F G : Sheaf J (Type _)` (SheafHom.lean:236) and
38	    `sheafHomSectionsEquiv : (sheafHom F G).1.sections ≃ (F ⟶ G)` (SheafHom.lean:241).
39	  - Separatedness companion: `CategoryTheory.eq_of_zeroHypercover_target`
40	    (MorphismProperty/Local.lean:189) — two morphisms equal on a cover are equal, given
41	    `(isomorphisms C).IsLocalAtTarget J`.
42	  Closest OBJECT-level descent in the module world: `SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.bind`
43	  (Sheaf/Quasicoherent.lean:358) glues quasicoherent *data* across `J.CoversTop` — proof that
44	  the descent machinery exists for module sheaves, but only for the QC-presentation structure,
45	  not arbitrary objects from a cocycle.
46	- **Project's path**: the blueprint wants compatible local isos through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`
47	  glued to a global morphism. There is **no `SheafOfModules R`-level packaging** of "hom is a
48	  sheaf"/amalgamation — `sheafHom` is for fixed-`A` `Sheaf J A`, not modules over a varying
49	  sheaf of rings.
50	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (small bridge). The genuine primitive (`Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom`)
51	  exists; the project must (i) forget to the underlying ab-sheaf via
52	  `SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` (Sheaf.lean:89, faithful + additive), glue the additive morphism
53	  with `sheafHom`/`sheafHomSectionsEquiv`, then (ii) promote to `𝒪_X`-linear via
54	  `PresheafOfModules.homMk` — R-linearity `f(r•m)=r•f(m)` is a sectionwise equation, so it
55	  holds globally once it holds on the cover (M separated). Est. ~30–60 LOC bridge.
56	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (the SheafOfModules-level wrapper), but built directly on
57	  the existing `Sites.SheafHom` primitive — NOT a from-scratch descent build.
58	
59	### Decision B: locally-iso ⇒ iso for sheaf-of-module morphisms
60	
61	- **Mathlib idiom**: `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`
62	  (Sites/LocallyBijective.lean:84): for `f : F ⟶ G` of `Sheaf J A`,
63	  `IsLocallyInjective f ∧ IsLocallySurjective f ↔ IsIso f`, given
64	  `[(forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]` + `[J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)]`.
65	  SheafOfModules side is already wired:
66	  - `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules.IsLocallyInjective`/`IsLocallySurjective` are DEFINED
67	    (Sheaf.lean:190,195) as the underlying ab-presheaf morphism being locally inj/surj.
68	  - `SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` reflects isos — used live as
69	    `isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R)` (Sheaf/Localization.lean:44).
70	  - `(forget R).ReflectsIsomorphisms` (Sheaf.lean:80).
71	  For `J = Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, `[J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrp]` +
72	  `HasSheafify` hold (the project already uses `W`-machinery on this site).
73	- **Project's path**: "iso is local" is already invoked informally; the right tool is the chain
74	  `IsIso f  ⇔  IsIso (toSheaf.map f)  ⇔  locally bijective`.
75	- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent + one connector. Missing piece: a lemma turning the project's
76	  "`M.restrict f` iso on an open cover" (restriction along an open *immersion*, base ring
77	  changes to `𝒪_U`) into the site-level `IsLocallyInjective J ∧ IsLocallySurjective J` on
78	  `Opens X`. Tractable (open-immersion restriction = `Over U` restriction of the ab-sheaf).

82	### Decision C: dual / internal-hom commuting with open-immersion restriction
83	
84	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE directly — Mathlib has no internal-hom/dual/`ihom` for
85	  `PresheafOfModules`/`SheafOfModules` (the project's presheaf `dual` is bespoke), hence no
86	  "`(dual M)|_U ≅ dual(M|_U)`". BUT the enabling ingredient is present and is exactly the H2
87	  half of the closed `tensorObj_restrict_iso`: `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv`
88	  (ChangeOfRings.lean:285) — restrictScalars along a ring **iso** is an **equivalence**
89	  (`isEquivalence_functor` :303), additive (:325) and linear (:335). Open-immersion restriction
90	  is sectionwise such a ring iso (`f.appIso`), so its `restrictScalars` is an equivalence and
91	  therefore commutes with `Hom(-,-)`/dual up to iso.
92	- **Project's path**: backs `dual_isLocallyTrivial`; build `(dual M)|_U ≅ dual_{𝒪_U}(M|_U)` by
93	  the SAME H1∘H2 recipe as `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (`pushforwardPushforwardAdj` /
94	  `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` + `restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv` carrying Hom).
95	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (bespoke, but the pieces are in hand and the ⊗ analogue is closed).
96	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — low/medium, mirror `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
97	
98	### Decision D: does the A+B+C route avoid the abandoned "d.2" stalk-⊗ gap?
99	
100	- **Mathlib idiom**: n/a — this is a route verdict.
101	- **Finding**: **YES, the descent route avoids d.2 and does not secretly re-require stalk-⊗.**
102	  - `tensorObj_restrict_iso` is CLOSED and **d.2-free** by construction: it goes through the
103	    pushforward/pullback adjunction (`pushforwardPushforwardAdj` + `leftAdjointUniq`) and the
104	    strong-monoidality of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion ring iso — **no stalks,
105	    no filtered-colimit-⊗, no whiskered-unit localizer**.
106	  - The local isos feeding the associator/inverse are assembled from `tensorObj_restrict_iso`
107	    + `tensorObj_unit_iso` + `tensorObjIsoOfIso` on trivialising opens — the EXACT pattern of
108	    the already-CLOSED `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (`:1912`), which touches no stalk.
109	  - A (gluing) and B (locally-iso⇒iso) are statements about MORPHISMS of (ab-)sheaves; neither
110	    computes a tensor stalk. The d.2 gap (`(F ⊗ᵖ M)_x ≅ F_x ⊗_{R_x} M_x` over the varying ring)
111	    is never invoked.
112	  - Contrast: the CURRENT `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (route (d)) needs
113	    `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` precisely because it manipulates the *iterated
114	    sheafification* `sheafify(η ▷ P)` directly — that whiskered-unit-stays-in-`W` step is what
115	    forces d.2. The descent route never forms that morphism; it builds the global iso from
116	    local trivialising data instead. **So the canonical-presheaf-construction route (associator

1514	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1515	
1516	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1517	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1518	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1519	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).

1522	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1523	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1524	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1525	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1526	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1527	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)

1530	
1531	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1532	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1533	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1534	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1535	
1536	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1537	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1538	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1539	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1540	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1541	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1542	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1543	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1544	
1545	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1546	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1547	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1548	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1549	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1550	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1551	law is definitional. -/
1552	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1553	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1554	
1555	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
1556	
1557	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
1558	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
1559	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
1560	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
1561	
1562	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
1563	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
1564	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
1565	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
1566	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
1567	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
1568	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
1569	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
1570	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
1571	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
1572	
1573	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
1574	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
1575	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
1576	
1577	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
1578	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
1579	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
1580	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1581	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1582	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
1583	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1584	
1585	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1586	
1587	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1588	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1589	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1590	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1591	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1592	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1593	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1594	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1595	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1596	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1597	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1598	
1599	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1600	
1601	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1602	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1603	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1604	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1605	
1606	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1607	
1608	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1609	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1610	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1611	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1612	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1613	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1614	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1615	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1616	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1617	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1618	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1619	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1620	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
1621	
1622	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1623	sheaf.**
1624	
1625	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1626	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1627	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1628	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1629	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1630	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1631	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1632	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
1633	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1634	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
1635	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
1636	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1637	
1638	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1639	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
1640	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
1641	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
1642	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
1643	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1644	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
1645	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1646	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1647	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1648	
1649	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1650	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
1651	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
1652	sheafification counit. -/
1653	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1654	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
1655	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1656	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1657	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M

2775	
2776	\begin{lemma}
2777	[Local isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global isomorphism]
2778	  \label{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}
2779	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict}
2780	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi \colon M \to N\) a morphism in
2781	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\).
2782	  Suppose that for every point \(x \in X\) there is an open neighbourhood \(U_x\) of
2783	  \(x\) such that the restriction of \(\varphi\) to \(U_x\) --- the image
2784	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) under the
2785	  restriction functor along the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\colon U_x \to X\) ---
2786	  is an isomorphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_x\). Then \(\varphi\) is an
2787	  isomorphism.
2788	\end{lemma}
2789	
2790	\begin{proof}
2791	  Being an isomorphism for a morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules can be
2792	  checked on the underlying morphism of sheaves of abelian groups, and there it can be
2793	  checked stalkwise. Fix a point \(x \in X\). Choose a preimage \(x' \in U_x\) of \(x\)
2794	  under the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\). By hypothesis the restriction
2795	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) is an
2796	  isomorphism, hence so is its image under any functor --- in particular under the
2797	  stalk-at-\(x'\) functor on the underlying ab-sheaves. The natural isomorphism
2798	  ``restriction commutes with stalks'' (\(\mathtt{restrictStalkNatIso}\)) identifies
2799	  this with the stalk of (the underlying ab-sheaf morphism of) \(\varphi\) at the image
2800	  point \(x\); transporting along it shows that stalk is an isomorphism. As this holds
2801	  at every \(x\), and a morphism of sheaves of abelian groups all of whose stalks are
2802	  isomorphisms is itself an isomorphism (stalkwise iso-detection,
2803	  \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_stalkFunctor\_map\_iso}\) on \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\)), the
2804	  underlying ab-sheaf morphism of \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism. Finally the forgetful
2805	  functor \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules.toPresheaf}\) reflects isomorphisms, so \(\varphi\)
2806	  itself is an isomorphism.
2807	\end{proof}
2808	
2809	\begin{lemma}
2810	[Compatible local morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global morphism]
2811	  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
2812	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
2813	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and \(\{U_i\}\) an
2814	  open cover of \(X\). Suppose given, for each \(i\), a morphism
2815	  \(f_i \colon M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_i\) such that
2816	  for all \(i, j\) the restrictions of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree.
2817	  Then there is a unique global morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
2818	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\).
2819	\end{lemma}
2820	
2821	\begin{proof}
2822	  A morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is, concretely, a morphism of the
2823	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is in addition \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear
2824	  sectionwise. We build \(f\) in two steps.
2825	
2826	  \emph{(i) Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.} Forget \(M\) and \(N\) to their
2827	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups via the faithful additive functor
2828	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.toSheaf}\). The assignment
2829	  \(U \mapsto \mathrm{Hom}(M|_U, N|_U)\) on the underlying ab-sheaves is itself a sheaf
2830	  --- this is the morphism-gluing / ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle
2831	  (\(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom}\) / \(\mathtt{sheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) in
2832	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom}\)). The given family \((f_i)\) is, after
2833	  forgetting to ab-sheaves, a compatible family of sections of this hom-sheaf over the
2834	  cover \(\{U_i\}\) (compatibility is exactly the agreement on the overlaps
2835	  \(U_i \cap U_j\)). By the sheaf condition it amalgamates to a unique global section,
2836	  i.e.\ a global morphism \(g\) of the underlying ab-sheaves whose restriction to each
2837	  \(U_i\) is the ab-sheaf morphism underlying \(f_i\).
2838	
2839	  \emph{(ii) Promote to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear.} The linearity equation
2840	  \(g(r \cdot m) = r \cdot g(m)\) is a sectionwise identity between two sections of a
2841	  separated presheaf. It holds on each \(U_i\), because there \(g\) restricts to the
2842	  module morphism \(f_i\); since the two sides agree on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) and the
2843	  ambient presheaf is separated, they agree globally. Packaging \(g\) together with this
2844	  global linearity yields a morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
2845	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) (via \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.homMk}\)). Its
2846	  restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\) and its uniqueness are both inherited from the
2847	  ab-sheaf amalgamation of step (i).
2848	
2849	  This descent step computes no tensor stalk --- it is purely a statement about gluing
2850	  morphisms of sheaves --- and so it does not re-enter the abandoned tensor-stalk
2851	  commutation.
2852	\end{proof}
2853	
2854	\begin{remark}
2855	[How the dual infrastructure discharges the \(\otimes\)-inverse]
2856	  \label{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}
2857	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial,
2858	        lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
2859	  Once \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,def:presheaf_dual,lem:internal_hom_eval,%
2860	  lem:internal_hom_isSheaf,lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} are available,
2861	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is discharged with no further missing
2862	  object: take \(L^{-1} := \mathtt{dual}\,L\) (\cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}), which
2863	  is a line bundle by \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}, and take \(\varepsilon_L\) to
2864	  be the descended evaluation of \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}. On a trivialising open
2865	  \(U\), the restriction-compatibility iso \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} carries
2866	  \((L \otimes_X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U\) and
2867	  \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) to the contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to
2868	  \mathcal{O}_U\) of the free rank-one module with its dual, which is the left unitor
2869	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and hence an isomorphism. As ``being an isomorphism''
2870	  is local on \(X\), the global \(\varepsilon_L\) is an isomorphism
2871	  \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\) by
2872	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} together with the structure-sheaf-unit comparison
2873	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}. This is the realization the
2874	  ``infrastructure-blocked'' note in \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} points
2875	  to.
2876	\end{remark}
2877	
2878	\begin{remark}
2879	[Alternative route via object-level descent (fallback)]
2880	  \label{rem:dual_via_stack}
2881	  Should the slice-and-sheafify route of
2882	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} bottom out, the dual can
2883	  instead be assembled by \emph{object-level descent}: glue the local trivial duals
2884	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) along the inverse-transpose transition isomorphisms
2885	  \(g_{ij}^{-\mathsf{T}}\) of \(L\) into a global \(L^{-1}\) on \(X\). Abstractly this
2886	  is effective descent for sheaves of modules on the small Zariski site --- the
2887	  essential-surjectivity of the descent-data functor of an \(\mathtt{IsStack}\)
2888	  pseudofunctor \(U \mapsto (\mathcal{O}_U\text{-modules})\). This is the more
2889	  principled and reusable construction but the larger build (it requires constructing
2890	  the restriction pseudofunctor and proving the stack/effective-descent property),
2891	  and is recorded only as a fallback; the slice internal-hom route above is the
2892	  intended one. It is the same morphism-/object-level descent family on which the
2893	  associator re-route of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} also waits.
2894	\end{remark}

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
32	
33	variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))

49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))

53	section Functor
54	
55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
79	
80	end Functor

82	variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}
83	
84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule
93	
94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119	
120	@[ext]
121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)
125	
126	lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M
127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]
136	
137	instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _
138	
139	@[simp, push ←]
140	lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
141	  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
142	  simp [← Hom.comp_app]
143	
144	end Presheaf

146	noncomputable section Functorial
147	
148	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)
149	
150	/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
151	def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
152	  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom
153	
154	@[simp]
155	lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
156	    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
157	
158	@[simp]
159	lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
160	    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl
161	
162	@[simp]
163	lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
164	    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
165	
166	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
167	def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
168	  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom
169	
170	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
171	is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
172	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
173	  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _
174	
175	section
176	
177	attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
178	  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts
179	
180	instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
181	instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
182	instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
183	instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
184	
185	end
186	
187	variable (X) in
188	/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
189	to the identity functor. -/
190	def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
191	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
192	
193	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
194	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
195	
196	variable (X) in
197	/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
198	to the identity functor. -/
199	def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
200	  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _
201	
202	variable (X) in
203	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
204	    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
205	      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
206	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _
207	
208	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
209	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
210	def pushforwardComp :
211	    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
212	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _
213	
214	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
215	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
216	
217	/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
218	identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
219	def pullbackComp :
220	    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
221	  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _
222	
223	/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
224	def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
225	    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
226	      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
227	
228	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
229	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
230	
231	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
232	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
233	
234	/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
235	def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
236	  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)
237	
238	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
239	    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
240	      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
241	    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
242	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _
243	
244	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
245	@[reassoc]
246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
247	    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
248	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
250	    eqToHom (by simp) := by
251	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
252	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
258	      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
259	  simp [this]
260	
261	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
262	@[reassoc]
263	lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
264	    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
265	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
266	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
267	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
268	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
269	  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
270	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
271	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
272	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
273	  simp [← this]
274	
275	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
276	@[reassoc]
277	lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
278	    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
279	      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
280	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
281	  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
282	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
283	  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
284	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
285	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
286	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
287	  simp [← this]
288	
289	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
290	attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
291	  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
292	  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
293	/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
294	a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
295	(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
296	these categories.) -/
297	@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
298	  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
299	  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
300	def pseudofunctor :
301	    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
302	  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
303	    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
304	    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
305	    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
306	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
307	    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
308	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))
309	
310	end Functorial
311	
312	noncomputable section Restriction
313	
314	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]
315	
316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
321	  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩
323	
324	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
325	abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
326	  (restrictFunctor f).obj M
327	
328	@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
329	    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl
330	
331	@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333	
334	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)
352	
353	instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
354	  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)
355	
356	instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
357	instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
358	instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
359	
360	@[simp]
361	lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
362	    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
363	      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl
364	
365	@[simp]
366	lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
367	    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
368	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl
369	
370	/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
371	def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
372	  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
373	
374	/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
375	def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
376	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
377	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
378	      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
379	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
380	
381	@[simp]
382	lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
383	    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
384	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl
385	
386	@[simp]
387	lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
388	    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
389	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl
390	
391	/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
392	def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
393	  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
394	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
395	    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
396	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
397	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
398	    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm
399	
400	@[simp]
401	lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
402	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
403	
404	@[simp]
405	lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
406	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
407	
408	/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
409	def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
410	    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
411	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
412	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)
413	
414	@[simp]
415	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
416	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
417	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
418	
419	@[simp]
420	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
421	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
422	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
423	
424	/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
425	def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :

10	/-! Internal hom of sheaves
11	
12	In this file, given two sheaves `F` and `G` on a site `(C, J)` with values
13	in a category `A`, we define a sheaf of types
14	`sheafHom F G` which sends `X : C` to the type of morphisms
15	between the restrictions of `F` and `G` to the categories `Over X`.
16	
17	We first define `presheafHom F G` when `F` and `G` are
18	presheaves `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` and show that it is a sheaf when `G` is a sheaf.
19	
20	TODO:

22	- for a sheaf of types `F`, the `sheafHom` functor from `F` is right-adjoint to
23	  the product functor with `F`, i.e. for all `X` and `Y`, there is a
24	  natural bijection `(X ⨯ F ⟶ Y) ≃ (X ⟶ sheafHom F Y)`.
25	- use these results in order to show that the category of sheaves of types is Cartesian closed
26	
27	-/
